cap_space_post_fa: TBD — approximately $10-15M remaining after Pierce re-sign and Jones tag; long-term extension with Jones would free meaningful space vs. the $37.833M tag number

key_departures.impact: First-round pick from 2021 who started 74 of 75 games over 5 seasons. Tallied only 4 sacks in 2025 — a career low for a full season — in an underwhelming contract year. Better run defender than pass rusher. Colts moved on as part of a full defensive line reset.
Rotational DT who posted 3.5 sacks in 2025 but struggled significantly against the run. Departure removes a serviceable interior piece but not an irreplaceable one.

Three-time team captain and longest-tenured Colt traded for DT Colby Wooden before free agency opened. Franklin was a full-time starter and one of the team's defensive signal-callers. His departure strips the LB room of its only proven veteran presence.

Colts traded their WR1 entering 2025 in a cap-driven move, sending Pittman (with a $29M cap hit) and a 7th-round pick to Pittsburgh in exchange for a 6th-round pick. The trade was made necessary by the cap cost of re-signing Alec Pierce. Pierce was already surpassing Pittman as the team's top target, so the net WR quality loss is moderate, not severe.

8-year Colts starter at right tackle departs after missing the final month of 2025 with a neck injury. His departure leaves a clear starter void at RT, though 2025 4th-round pick Jalen Travis filled in capably (74.2 PFF grade, 1 sack allowed in 165 pass-blocking snaps) down the stretch.

Young, ascending starter who tallied 145+ tackles and three interceptions in 2025. Cap constraints with Cam Bynum already on a 4yr/$60M deal made re-signing both safeties financially difficult. His departure opens a starting hole alongside Bynum. 2025 rookie Hunter Wohler (missed season with foot injury) is the in-house heir apparent.

Mid-season addition in 2025 who stepped in as a co-starter at LB alongside Franklin. With Franklin traded and Pratt likely departing, the Colts enter 2026 with essentially no proven LBs on the roster. A critical compounding departure given the Franklin trade.

Veteran EDGE who missed 3 games with a knee injury in 2025 and never fully regained his pre-Achilles form. One of multiple edge rushers likely departing, further thinning a group that already lost Paye.
Rotational edge rusher entering final year of 2-year deal; status unclear. Low-end impact loss if he departs, but adds to the broader edge depth problem.

signings.analysis: The Colts' most important offseason transaction. Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch (21.3) for the second consecutive year in 2025 and surpassed 1,000 receiving yards for the first time. The 4yr/$114M deal ($84M in guarantees, $60M fully guaranteed at signing) is a clear elite-tier investment — top-10 WR money. Retaining Pierce directly enabled the Pittman trade, shifting him from WR2 to WR1. With Josh Downs in the slot and Tyler Warren at TE, the receiving corps remains functional despite losing Pittman. WR1 need is resolved; WR depth behind Pierce is a secondary concern.

contract.notes: Transition tag only as of 2026-03-11. Long-term extension negotiations ongoing — progress reported by Jordan Schultz on March 10, 2026 but no deal finalized. Colts initial offer was ~3yr/$100.5M range; Jones camp sought $50M/yr. Deadline to agree on extension is July 15, 2026.

signings.expected_role: starter (recovering from torn Achilles, suffered December 2025)
signings.analysis: The transition tag keeps Jones tethered to Indianapolis and gives Colts matching rights on any outside offer sheet, but a long-term deal has NOT been finalized as of today. At $37.8M on the tag, QB is expensive and unresolved long-term. Jones was highly effective before his December Achilles injury — the Colts clearly want him back — but injury risk and contract gap ($35M/yr Colts offer vs. $50M/yr Jones ask) create real uncertainty. This is a partially-addressed situation: Jones will likely start in 2026, but the position lacks long-term certainty. The Colts cannot fully close the QB need until an extension is signed.

contract.notes: Up to $20M max value per NFL.com; $11M guaranteed per Ian Rapoport

signings.analysis: Key's 2yr/$20M deal is below the starter threshold for EDGE ($15M+ AAV). A career journeyman who has never broken out as a full-time starter, his career-high is 6.5 sacks (done twice). He is not an upgrade on the departing Kwity Paye and will slot in as a rotational complement to Laiatu Latu and JT Tuimoloau. Per ESPN, his signing won't preclude the Colts from adding additional edge players in free agency or the draft. EDGE remains a critical need — this signing is a floor, not a ceiling.

signings.previous_team: Green Bay Packers (trade for Zaire Franklin)

contract.notes: Acquired in trade; playing on existing rookie deal. No new money committed.

signings.analysis: Young IDL acquired as part of the Zaire Franklin trade. Known primarily as a run-stopper with developmental upside. Does not offset Gallimore's departure at a high level, but adds a young body with team control. IDL depth remains thin — this is a low-cost addition, not a solution.

contract.notes: Re-signed on 1yr deal worth up to $1.4M per NFL.com. Exclusive rights free agent.

signings.expected_role: starter (competing with Spencer Shrader returning from ACL/MCL)
signings.analysis: Grupe was a late-season find in 2025, going 11/11 on field goals including a 60-yard franchise record. Re-signing him on a minimal deal is a no-brainer. Kicker was not a pre-FA need.

contract.notes: Signed January 5, 2026 as exclusive rights free agent

signings.analysis: Exclusive rights tender locks in a depth LB/special teams contributor. Posted 32 tackles in 2025. Does not address the starting LB void left by Franklin and Pratt departures.

contract.notes: Re-signed; specific terms not publicly reported as of 2026-03-11. Likely a low-cost deal given his depth/ST role.

signings.analysis: Core special teams player and depth WR entering his 7th year with the Colts. Second-team AP All-Pro special teamer in 2021. Re-signing him provides continuity on special teams and WR depth behind Pierce and Downs. Low-cost, low-impact roster management move.

contract.notes: Re-signed; terms not publicly reported. Depth/backup role.

signings.analysis: Veteran backup center/guard who made one emergency start in 2025. Re-signing him ensures interior line depth. No impact on positional need picture.

contract.notes: Re-signed; terms not publicly reported. Depth role.

signings.analysis: Veteran backup who made 3 starts at center during the 2023 season. Continuity depth signing, no impact on primary needs.

signings.previous_team: External free agent (previous team undisclosed in available sources)

contract.notes: Terms not publicly reported as of 2026-03-11

signings.analysis: Depth cornerback addition. Charvarius Ward (already under contract, year 2 of 3yr/$54M) and Sauce Gardner anchor the CB room. This is a low-cost depth/special teams add, not a starter-level signing.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Alec Pierce re-signing on a 4yr/$114M deal ($84M gtd, $60M fully guaranteed at signing) fully resolves the WR1 need. Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch in 2025 and is now the unquestioned WR1 with an elite-tier contract commitment. Josh Downs remains as a quality slot option and Tyler Warren as TE1. The Pittman trade does create a WR2 vacancy but the offense remains functional. A Day 3 WR pick is reasonable; WR is no longer a priority need.

Daniel Jones is retained on the transition tag ($37.833M) and is expected to start in 2026 once he recovers from his December Achilles tear. A long-term extension is in active negotiation and progress has been reported, but nothing is finalized as of 2026-03-11. The position is de-escalated from tier 1 because Jones will be the starter, but it remains a tier 2 need due to: (1) no guaranteed long-term commitment yet, (2) Achilles injury recovery uncertainty, (3) unresolved contract gap. If an extension is signed, QB drops to tier 3-4.

The edge situation has worsened significantly. Kwity Paye departed (Raiders, 3yr/$48M), Samson Ebukam is unsigned/departing, and Tyquan Lewis is uncertain. The only proven edge rushers under contract are Laiatu Latu (2024 1st-rounder needing more help) and JT Tuimoloau (2025 2nd-rounder). Arden Key's 2yr/$20M deal is rotational-depth tier, not a starter solution. With potentially three of the top four 2025 edge rushers gone, EDGE becomes the single biggest remaining draft and free agency need — escalate to tier 1.

Catastrophic LB depletion. Zaire Franklin was traded (for Colby Wooden), Germaine Pratt is unsigned and likely departed, and only ERFA depth piece Austin Ajiake and unproven converted safety Jaylon Carlies remain. The Colts enter the draft with essentially zero proven LB starters. Lou Anarumo's defense requires functional linebackers. This is a co-tier-1 need with EDGE — potentially the most pressing need on the entire roster given the complete roster depletion at the position.

Nick Cross (starter-level, ascending, 145+ tackles in 2025) has not been re-signed and appears headed elsewhere due to cap constraints with Camryn Bynum already on a 4yr/$60M deal. Cam Bynum remains a quality starter, and 2025 rookie Hunter Wohler (missed season with foot injury) is the in-house heir apparent at SS. The hole is real but has a credible developmental answer. S should rise from tier 3 to tier 2 — worth addressing in Day 2-3 of the draft or cheap free agency.

Braden Smith signed with the Houston Texans, leaving the Colts without their veteran RT. However, 2025 4th-round pick Jalen Travis earned a 74.2 PFF grade in relief duty and is the expected starter going forward. Travis's competence mitigates the urgency, but he has only 4 starts of NFL experience. OT rises to tier 2 — the Colts should consider a veteran RT insurance policy or a developmental OT in the draft, but this is not a top priority.

Neville Gallimore departed to the Bears, leaving IDL depth thin. DeForest Buckner (neck surgery/IR in 2025, recovery pending) and Grover Stewart are the primary IDL starters but both are 30+. Colby Wooden (acquired in Franklin trade) is young but unproven. With Buckner's health uncertain, IDL needs to be addressed — at minimum a quality rotational body, ideally a developmental starter.

key_additions.headline: NFL's top deep threat re-signs on elite deal, becoming undisputed WR1 after Pittman trade
Transition tag retains Jones for 2026 as he recovers from Achilles; long-term extension in negotiation but not finalized
Veteran rotational edge rusher adds depth behind Laiatu Latu but does not fill the starter void left by Paye

key_additions.contract_summary: Acquired via trade (Zaire Franklin sent to Packers)
key_additions.headline: Young IDL run-stopper acquired in Franklin trade provides developmental interior depth

key_losses.headline: 5-year Colts starter and 2021 first-round pick departs after underwhelming 4-sack 2025 season
Three-time team captain and starting LB traded pre-free agency, gutting an already thin linebacker room

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded (7th-round pick swap) then signed 3yr/$59M with Pittsburgh
key_losses.headline: Cap-driven trade clears $24M to fund the Pierce re-signing; Pittman had 784 yards and 7 TDs in 2025
8-year Colts right tackle departs; 2025 4th-rounder Jalen Travis expected to start in his place
Ascending 24-year-old safety (145+ tackles in 2025) likely priced out by Bynum's existing deal
Rotational IDL departs, further thinning a defensive interior already reliant on aging veterans

confidence_notes: Contract figures for Pierce (NFL.com/Rapoport/Pelissero), Jones transition tag (Spotrac/NFL.com), Key (NFL.com/Rapoport), Bynum (OTC/Spotrac — 2025 signing, on roster), Ward (OTC/Spotrac — 2025 signing, on roster), Grupe (NFL.com) are all sourced from Tier 1 outlets. Ashton Dulin, Danny Pinter, Wesley French re-sign terms are confirmed by Colts.com but specific financial terms not reported. Corey Ballentine terms undisclosed. Nick Cross, Germaine Pratt, Samson Ebukam, Tyquan Lewis statuses are UFA-unsigned as of 2026-03-11 — confirmed departed only for Paye, Gallimore, Franklin, Pittman, and Smith. Daniel Jones long-term deal NOT confirmed — tag only.
